libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: If tests exercise method interception, proxy generation, or com.google.inject.internal.cglib paths, configure the test JVM with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`; also pass the same `--add-opens` as a native-image build argument when those paths are covered. For the 2.9.1 javac failure specifically, do not reuse 2.1.7 internal imports blindly: many shaded internal classes are renamed with a `$` prefix in 2.9.1, e.g. `com.google.inject.internal.util.$MapMaker`, `.$FinalizableReferenceQueue`, `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.$MethodProxy`, and `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.core.$Signature`.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Tests that rely on `com.google.inject.internal.*` are brittle across sisu-guice versions; public Guice APIs such as `Guice.createInjector`, `AbstractModule`, bindings, injection points, providers, and method interceptors are safer where they still reach the required metadata.

Without the `--add-opens` JVM/native-image argument, CGLIB-based method interception can fail on modern JDKs with an inaccessible `ClassLoader.defineClass` reflection error even though basic injection works.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed; the artifact is self-contained apart from normal transitive javax.inject/aopalliance dependencies, but meaningful Guice AOP/proxy coverage on modern JDKs needs java.lang opened for the shaded CGLIB code.
